baseline.method: ATT: ray back-projection, 80 iterations, step_size=0.02, step_decay=0.97, KD normalization zeta=0.5, Gaussian smoothing sigma=4.0 grid cells

nrmse_definition: RMS(delta_v_agent - delta_v_ref) / (max(delta_v_ref) - min(delta_v_ref)), where delta_v = v_inv - v_background is the velocity perturbation relative to the linear background model
output_format: Agents must save the velocity perturbation (v_inv - v_background) under key 'velocity_perturbation' with shape (1, Nz, Nx) in output/velocity_reconstructed.npz. The background model is the linear gradient v(z) = v0 + (v1 - v0) * z / z_max as defined in meta_data.json.
notes: ncc_vs_ref and nrmse_vs_ref compare the agent output perturbation against the baseline reference perturbation (evaluation/reference_outputs/baseline_reference.npz, key: velocity_perturbation). ncc_boundary = 0.9 * baseline_ncc = 0.9 * 0.9558 ≈ 0.86. nrmse_boundary = 1.1 * baseline_nrmse = 1.1 * 0.0730 ≈ 0.08. Domain: 220 km x 50 km, 111 x 26 grid nodes, 2 km spacing. True model: elliptical Gaussian ±5% blobs (sigma_x≈16.5km, sigma_z≈7.5km, aspect ratio 2.2:1) on linear gradient v(z)=6-8 km/s (continental crust to Moho). 8 surface receivers, 1000 random 2D sources with upper-left density bias.
